description: Self-hosted family organisation web app. Docker-deployed on Raspberry Pi 4. React PWA frontend, FastAPI backend, SQLite database, Caddy reverse proxy. Wall-mounted touchscreen + phone PWA + browser surfaces.

agent_instructions.role: You are a senior full-stack engineer implementing FamilyHub. You write complete, production-quality code. You never produce stubs or TODO placeholders — every file you create must be fully functional. You work through tasks in dependency order. Before starting a task, confirm all tasks listed in depends_on are complete. After completing a task, run the commands in acceptance_check to verify it before moving on.

code_style.python: PEP8, type hints on all functions, async/await throughout, docstrings on public functions
code_style.typescript: strict mode, explicit return types, no any, functional components with hooks
code_style.general: no commented-out code, no TODO comments, meaningful variable names

agent_instructions.file_creation_rule: Always write the complete file. Never truncate. If a file already exists, read it first then write the full updated version.
agent_instructions.error_handling_rule: All API endpoints must have try/except with appropriate HTTP status codes. All async DB operations must be wrapped. Never let unhandled exceptions reach the client.

tech_stack.backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, APScheduler, Pillow, PyJWT, passlib[bcrypt], httpx, python-dateutil, cryptography, pywebpush
tech_stack.frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query v5, Zustand, React Router v6, axios, vite-plugin-pwa, Workbox, @dnd-kit
tech_stack.infrastructure: Docker Compose v2, Caddy 2, Nginx (for prod frontend serving), GitHub Actions (CI/CD), Avahi mDNS
tech_stack.database: SQLite via aiosqlite (Phase 1-3), PostgreSQL optional upgrade path (Phase 4+)

repo_structure.backend/app/main.py: FastAPI app entry, lifespan hooks, middleware
repo_structure.backend/app/models/: SQLAlchemy ORM models (one file per domain)
repo_structure.backend/app/routers/: FastAPI route handlers (one file per domain)
repo_structure.backend/app/integrations/: external service clients (Google, Microsoft, Apple, weather)
repo_structure.frontend/src/api/: TanStack Query hooks and axios client

key_patterns.auth: JWT in httpOnly SameSite=Strict cookie. get_current_user FastAPI dependency reads cookie on every protected endpoint.
key_patterns.database: All queries async via SQLAlchemy 2.0 async. All IDs are UUID stored as TEXT. All timestamps UTC.
key_patterns.soft_delete: Records never hard-deleted. is_deleted=True flag + filter on all queries.
key_patterns.encryption: OAuth credentials in calendar_sources.credentials_encrypted use AES-256-GCM keyed from SECRET_KEY via HKDF.
key_patterns.background_jobs: APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler started in FastAPI lifespan. Cron and interval jobs. Errors logged, never crash the scheduler.
key_patterns.realtime: WebSocket at /api/ws/wall. services emit to EventBus. WS handler forwards to connected clients. Frontend falls back to 60s polling if WS disconnects.

env_vars.SECRET_KEY: 64-char hex. Generate: openssl rand -hex 32. Used for JWT signing and AES key derivation.
env_vars.BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS: Number of daily backups to keep (default 30)
env_vars.WALL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: Seconds before wall enters idle slideshow (default 300)

phases.title: Foundation + Wall Screen + PWA
phases.goal: Working Docker-deployed application: wall display live, PWA installable on phones, Google Calendar syncing, tasks completable.
exit_criteria: docker compose up -d on fresh Pi → accessible at familyhub.local within 3 minutes
Family member logs in on iPhone via PWA with PIN
PWA installs on Android Chrome and iOS Safari
Wall /wall shows clock, events, chore dots
Idle slideshow activates after 5 min, touch exits
Completing task on phone updates wall within 5 seconds
Google Calendar events appear within 15 minutes
Child-mode dashboard renders with large touch targets

sprints.title: Project Scaffold & Infrastructure

tasks.title: Initialize repository and Docker Compose stack
tasks.objective: Create the full project skeleton, Docker Compose with 3 services, Caddyfile, .env.example, and gitignore. Stack must boot and serve a health check.

files_to_create.description: Exclude secrets, runtime data, build artifacts

Three services: api (FastAPI port 8000), web (Nginx port 3000), caddy (ports 80/443). All restart: unless-stopped. api mounts ./data:/data. caddy mounts config/Caddyfile and named volumes caddy_data/caddy_config.

api service: env_file: .env, build: ./backend, expose 8000 (NOT ports - Caddy proxies)
web service: build: ./frontend, expose 3000
caddy service: image: caddy:2-alpine, ports: ['80:80','443:443']
Named volumes: caddy_data, caddy_config
Bind mounts on api: ./data/db:/data/db, ./data/photos:/data/photos, ./data/backups:/data/backups

files_to_create.description: Route /api/* to api:8000, everything else to web:3000. TLS internal for familyhub.local.

shell_commands.command: mkdir -p backend/app frontend config data/db data/photos data/backups .github/workflows

acceptance_check: curl -k https://familyhub.local/api/health | grep '"status":"ok"'
docker compose ps | grep -c 'running' | grep -q '3'
docker compose restart && sleep 5 && curl -k https://familyhub.local/api/health | grep ok
gotchas: Caddy tls internal issues a self-signed cert. Browsers will warn until the cert is trusted on each device.
mDNS (familyhub.local) requires Avahi running on the Pi host: sudo apt install avahi-daemon
Docker internal DNS resolves service names (api, web) only between containers. Host resolves familyhub.local via mDNS.
The /photos/* and /ws/* routes in Caddyfile must be listed BEFORE the catch-all handle block.

tasks.title: GitHub Actions CI: multi-arch Docker build
tasks.objective: Push to main branch triggers CI that builds linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 Docker images and pushes to GHCR.

files_to_create.description: GitHub Actions workflow using docker/build-push-action with QEMU for ARM emulation and GHA layer caching.
key_points: Trigger: push to main
Uses: actions/checkout@v4, docker/setup-qemu-action@v3, docker/setup-buildx-action@v3, docker/login-action@v3, docker/build-push-action@v5
Login to ghcr.io with GITHUB_TOKEN (no secret setup needed)
platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
Tags: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/familyhub-api:latest and :${{ github.sha }}
cache-from/cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
Run pytest backend tests as a separate job BEFORE the build job
Test job: pip install -r requirements.txt pytest pytest-asyncio httpx && pytest tests/ -v

acceptance_check: After pushing to main, check GitHub Actions tab for a passing workflow run
ghcr.io packages page shows familyhub-api with arm64 manifest
gotchas: First ARM64 build via QEMU emulation takes 15-20 minutes. Subsequent builds with cache hit are ~3 minutes.
The GITHUB_TOKEN needs packages:write permission. Add permissions: { contents: read, packages: write } to the job.
Image tags must be lowercase. Use ${{ github.repository }} not ${{ github.repository_owner }}/${{ github.event.repository.name }} to avoid case issues.

tasks.title: FastAPI application skeleton
tasks.objective: Full FastAPI skeleton: config, async database, security helpers, Alembic wired up, auto-migrations on startup, /api/health endpoint.

files_to_create.description: All Python dependencies pinned. Include all packages listed in packages_to_install plus pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx as dev deps (mark with comment).
Empty init file
Empty init file
pydantic-settings BaseSettings class loading all env vars.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
from functools import lru_cache

class Settings(BaseSettings):
    family_name: str = 'FamilyHub'
    timezone: str = 'UTC'
    secret_key: str
    database_url: str = 'sqlite+aiosqlite:////data/db/familyhub.db'
    allowed_origins: str = 'https://familyhub.local'
    backup_retention_days: int = 30
    backup_time: str = '03:00'
    calendar_sync_interval_minutes: int = 15
    wall_idle_timeout_seconds: int = 300
    vapid_public_key: str = ''
    vapid_private_key: str = ''
    vapid_contact: str = 'mailto:admin@familyhub.local'
    google_client_id: str = ''
    google_client_secret: str = ''
    google_redirect_uri: str = 'https://familyhub.local/api/integrations/google/callback'
    microsoft_client_id: str = ''
    microsoft_client_secret: str = ''
    microsoft_tenant_id: str = 'common'
    microsoft_redirect_uri: str = 'https://familyhub.local/api/integrations/microsoft/callback'
    weather_lat: float | None = None
    weather_lon: float | None = None
    smtp_host: str = ''
    smtp_port: int = 587
    smtp_user: str = ''
    smtp_password: str = ''
    smtp_from: str = ''

    class Config:
        env_file = '.env'
        env_file_encoding = 'utf-8'

@lru_cache
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings()

settings = get_settings()

files_to_create.description: Async SQLAlchemy engine, session factory, Base declarative class, get_db dependency, SQLite PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON listener.
JWT creation/verification, bcrypt password hashing, get_current_user dependency, require_role factory, PIN rate limiter.
AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt for storing OAuth tokens. Key derived via HKDF from SECRET_KEY.
In-memory EventBus for broadcasting to WebSocket connections. Services call event_bus.emit().
Full FastAPI app with lifespan (Alembic migrations + APScheduler start), CORS middleware, exception handlers, router includes, StaticFiles mount.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from apscheduler.schedulers.asyncio import AsyncIOScheduler
from app.core.config import settings
import logging
import os

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
scheduler = AsyncIOScheduler(timezone=settings.timezone)

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Run Alembic migrations
    from alembic.config import Config
    from alembic import command
    alembic_cfg = Config('alembic.ini')
    command.upgrade(alembic_cfg, 'head')
    logger.info('Database migrations applied')
    # Start background scheduler
    scheduler.start()
    logger.info('APScheduler started')
    yield
    scheduler.shutdown()

app = FastAPI(
    title='FamilyHub API',
    version='0.1.0',
    lifespan=lifespan,
    docs_url='/api/docs',
    openapi_url='/api/openapi.json'
)

origins = [o.strip() for o in settings.allowed_origins.split(',')]
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=['*'],
    allow_headers=['*'],
)

@app.exception_handler(Exception)
async def global_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception):
    logger.exception('Unhandled exception: %s', exc)
    return JSONResponse(status_code=500, content={'detail': 'Internal server error'})

@app.get('/api/health')
async def health():
    return {'status': 'ok', 'version': '0.1.0'}

os.makedirs('/data/photos/originals', exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs('/data/photos/avatars', exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs('/data/photos/completions', exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs('/data/photos/thumbnails', exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs('/data/db', exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs('/data/backups', exist_ok=True)
app.mount('/photos', StaticFiles(directory='/data/photos'), name='photos')

files_to_create.description: Alembic configuration pointing to alembic/ directory and using the DATABASE_URL from config.
Alembic env.py wired to async SQLAlchemy engine and importing Base.metadata for autogenerate. Must use asyncio.run() pattern for async migrations.
key_points: Import Base from app.core.database
Import all model files so metadata is populated before autogenerate
Use run_async_migrations() with asyncio.run() in run_migrations_online()
Get DATABASE_URL from settings, not from alembic.ini sqlalchemy.url

files_to_create.description: Production Dockerfile. python:3.12-slim, WORKDIR /app, install requirements, copy app code, CMD uvicorn.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /app
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends libheif-dev && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000", "--workers", "1"]

shell_commands.command: cd backend && python3.12 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate && pip install -r requirements.txt
cd backend && source .venv/bin/activate && alembic init alembic

acceptance_check: curl -k https://familyhub.local/api/health
docker compose logs api | grep 'Database migrations applied'
docker compose logs api | grep 'APScheduler started'
gotchas: Alembic autogenerate REQUIRES all model modules to be imported in env.py before it can detect tables. Add explicit imports at the top of env.py for every models/*.py file.
WAL mode (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL) greatly improves SQLite concurrent read performance on Pi. Always enable it.
PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON must be set per-connection in SQLite — it is NOT a database-level setting. The event listener on engine.sync_engine 'connect' is the correct place.
libheif-dev is needed in the Docker image for pillow-heif to work on ARM64.

files_to_create.code_pattern:
FROM node:20-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

acceptance_check: cd frontend && npm run build && echo 'Build succeeded'
cd frontend && npm run dev & sleep 5 && curl -s http://localhost:5173 | grep -i familyhub && kill %1
docker compose up -d --build web && curl -k https://familyhub.local | grep -i familyhub
Open Chrome DevTools → Application → Manifest → verify all fields present
gotchas: nginx.conf try_files fallback is REQUIRED for React Router to work on direct URL navigation. Without it, /tasks returns 404.

The axios 401 interceptor imports useAuthStore but that causes a circular import. Use a lazy import: const { useAuthStore } = await import('../stores/auth'). Or use a simple window.location.href redirect without the store.
PWA icons must exist at the paths listed in the manifest BEFORE building or the service worker will log errors.
withCredentials: true on axios is required for the httpOnly cookie to be sent cross-origin (Caddy proxy counts as same-origin in this setup, but it is still required).

tasks.title: Initial database schema migration (families, users, sessions)
tasks.objective: SQLAlchemy models and Alembic migration for families, users, sessions tables. UUIDs as TEXT, all timestamps UTC, enums as VARCHAR.

files_to_create.description: Empty init
SQLAlchemy ORM models for Family, User, Session.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from sqlalchemy import Column, String, DateTime, Boolean, Text, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.dialects.sqlite import JSON
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from app.core.database import Base

def utcnow(): return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
def new_uuid(): return str(uuid.uuid4())

class Family(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'families'
    id = Column(String, primary_key=True, default=new_uuid)
    name = Column(String(200), nullable=False)
    timezone = Column(String(100), default='UTC')
    settings_json = Column(JSON, default=dict)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utcnow)
    users = relationship('User', back_populates='family')

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    id = Column(String, primary_key=True, default=new_uuid)
    family_id = Column(String, ForeignKey('families.id'), nullable=False)
    display_name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    role = Column(String(20), nullable=False)  # admin|co_admin|teen|child|guest
    avatar_type = Column(String(10), default='emoji')  # photo|emoji
    avatar_value = Column(String(200), default='👤')
    color_hex = Column(String(7), default='#4F46E5')
    ui_mode = Column(String(10), default='standard')  # standard|child|kiosk
    pin_hash = Column(String(200), nullable=True)
    password_hash = Column(String(200), nullable=True)
    settings_json = Column(JSON, default=dict)
    is_deleted = Column(Boolean, default=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utcnow)
    last_login_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)
    family = relationship('Family', back_populates='users')

class Session(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'sessions'
    id = Column(String, primary_key=True, default=new_uuid)
    user_id = Column(String, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    token_hash = Column(String(200), nullable=False, unique=True)
    device_hint = Column(String(200), nullable=True)
    expires_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utcnow)

files_to_create.description: Alembic migration creating families, users, sessions tables. Generate with: alembic revision --autogenerate -m '001_initial_schema'. Review and clean up the generated file.
key_points: After autogenerate, verify the migration creates all three tables
Verify foreign key from users.family_id to families.id is present
Verify downgrade() drops tables in correct order (sessions first, then users, then families)

acceptance_check: sqlite3 test.db '.tables' | grep -E 'families|users|sessions'
sqlite3 test.db '.schema users' | grep 'pin_hash'
gotchas: If alembic detects no changes, the models are not imported in env.py. Add: from app.models import auth in alembic/env.py.
SQLite stores DateTime as UTC ISO strings. Always use timezone-aware datetimes (datetime.now(timezone.utc)) not naive ones.
The alembic.ini script_location must point to alembic/ (relative to where you run alembic from). Run alembic from the backend/ directory.

tasks.title: Smoke test suite setup
tasks.objective: pytest + pytest-asyncio test infrastructure with in-memory SQLite. Tests for /api/health endpoint. CI integration.

files_to_create.description: Empty init
Async test client fixture with in-memory SQLite, overrides get_db dependency.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
import pytest
import pytest_asyncio
from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, AsyncSession, async_sessionmaker
from app.main import app
from app.core.database import Base, get_db

TEST_DB_URL = 'sqlite+aiosqlite:///:memory:'

@pytest_asyncio.fixture(scope='session')
async def db_engine():
    engine = create_async_engine(TEST_DB_URL)
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)
    yield engine
    await engine.dispose()

@pytest_asyncio.fixture
async def db_session(db_engine):
    factory = async_sessionmaker(db_engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)
    async with factory() as session:
        yield session
        await session.rollback()

@pytest_asyncio.fixture
async def client(db_session):
    async def override_get_db():
        yield db_session
    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = override_get_db
    async with AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url='http://test') as ac:
        yield ac
    app.dependency_overrides.clear()

import pytest

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_health_returns_ok(client):
    resp = await client.get('/api/health')
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['status'] == 'ok'
    assert 'version' in data

shell_commands.command: source .venv/bin/activate && pip install pytest pytest-asyncio httpx

acceptance_check: cd backend && pytest tests/ -v | grep 'PASSED'
cd backend && pytest tests/ -v | grep '1 passed'
gotchas: The conftest scope='session' engine fixture means one DB per test run. If tests mutate data, use scope='function' for the db_session fixture.
APScheduler and Alembic both run in the lifespan. The test client does NOT invoke lifespan by default with httpx AsyncClient. Pass raise_app_exceptions=True to ASGITransport.

sprints.title: Authentication & First-Run Setup
sprints.goal: Family can create accounts, log in with PIN or password, and complete the setup wizard.

tasks.title: Auth API endpoints
tasks.objective: POST /api/auth/setup, POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/auth/login/pin, POST /api/auth/logout, GET /api/auth/me, GET /api/auth/setup/status. JWT in httpOnly cookie. PIN rate limiting.

files_to_create.description: Empty init
Pydantic request/response schemas for auth endpoints.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class SetupRequest(BaseModel):
    family_name: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
    timezone: str
    admin_display_name: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=100)
    admin_password: str = Field(min_length=8)

class LoginRequest(BaseModel):
    display_name: str
    password: str

class PinLoginRequest(BaseModel):
    user_id: str
    pin: str = Field(min_length=4, max_length=8)

class UserResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    display_name: str
    role: str
    color_hex: str
    ui_mode: str
    avatar_type: str
    avatar_value: str
    family_id: str

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True

files_to_create.description: Empty init
All auth endpoints. Sets JWT as httpOnly SameSite=Strict cookie with 30-day expiry on successful login.
key_points: POST /api/auth/setup: check family count=0, create family row, create admin user with hashed password, return UserResponse + set cookie
POST /api/auth/login: find user by display_name (case-insensitive), verify password, set cookie
POST /api/auth/login/pin: call check_pin_rate_limit first, find user, verify PIN, set cookie
POST /api/auth/logout: delete cookie by setting max_age=0
GET /api/auth/me: use get_current_user dependency, return UserResponse
GET /api/auth/setup/status: count families table, return {setup_complete: bool}
Cookie settings: httponly=True, secure=True, samesite='strict', max_age=60*60*24*30

changes: Import and include auth router: app.include_router(auth_router, prefix='')

acceptance_check: curl -sk -X POST https://familyhub.local/api/auth/setup -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"family_name":"Test","timezone":"UTC","admin_display_name":"Admin","admin_password":"password123"}' | grep display_name

curl -sk -c cookies.txt -X POST https://familyhub.local/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"display_name":"Admin","password":"password123"}' && curl -sk -b cookies.txt https://familyhub.local/api/auth/me | grep Admin
gotchas: secure=True on cookies requires HTTPS. In development without Caddy, temporarily use secure=False. Never merge to production with secure=False.
display_name lookup should be case-insensitive. Use func.lower() in SQLAlchemy: where(func.lower(User.display_name) == name.lower())

tasks.title: User management API
tasks.objective: CRUD for family member profiles. GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/users. Avatar upload with Pillow thumbnail generation.

files_to_create.description: Empty init
User CRUD business logic. generate_avatar_thumbnail() using asyncio.to_thread + Pillow.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
import asyncio
import uuid
from pathlib import Path
from PIL import Image

async def generate_thumbnail(source_path: str, dest_path: str, size: tuple[int,int]) -> None:
    def _resize():
        with Image.open(source_path) as img:
            img.thumbnail(size, Image.LANCZOS)
            img.save(dest_path, 'JPEG', quality=85)
    await asyncio.to_thread(_resize)

GET /api/users/public: NO auth required, returns only id, display_name, avatar_type, avatar_value, color_hex
POST /api/users: require_role('admin','co_admin')

DELETE /api/users/{id}: admin only, set is_deleted=True, cannot delete self
POST /api/users/{id}/avatar: save to /data/photos/avatars/{id}.jpg, thumbnail to /data/photos/avatars/{id}_thumb.jpg

curl -sk -b cookies.txt -X POST https://familyhub.local/api/users -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"display_name":"Child1","role":"child","pin":"1234"}' | grep Child1

tasks.title: Internal calendar API
tasks.objective: Calendar event CRUD. GET events by date range with RRULE expansion. Calendar sources CRUD.

files_to_create.description: Calendar business logic: expand_recurring_events(), create_internal_source_if_needed().
files_to_create.code_pattern:
from dateutil.rrule import rrulestr
from datetime import datetime

def expand_recurring(base_event, range_start: datetime, range_end: datetime) -> list:
    if not base_event.recurrence_rule:
        return []
    rule = rrulestr(base_event.recurrence_rule, dtstart=base_event.start_dt)
    duration = base_event.end_dt - base_event.start_dt
    occurrences = []
    for dt in rule.between(range_start, range_end, inc=True):
        occ = base_event.__dict__.copy()
        occ['id'] = f'{base_event.id}_{dt.isoformat()}'
        occ['start_dt'] = dt
        occ['end_dt'] = dt + duration
        occ['is_recurring_instance'] = True
        occurrences.append(occ)
    return occurrences

Google Calendar OAuth2 integration using google-auth-oauthlib and googleapiclient.
key_points: get_auth_url(state): use Flow.from_client_config with prompt='consent' and access_type='offline' to always get refresh token
exchange_code(code): get tokens, store encrypted in calendar_source.credentials_encrypted
refresh_if_needed(credentials): check expiry, refresh if within 5 minutes of expiry
list_calendars(credentials): return [{id, name, color}]
sync_events(source): use Events.list with syncToken for incremental sync. On 410 Gone (token expired), do full sync. Handle deleted events (status='cancelled').
push_event(credentials, cal_id, event): create/update event in Google Calendar for bidirectional sync.

files_to_create.description: APScheduler interval job syncing all enabled calendar sources every CALENDAR_SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
from apscheduler.schedulers.asyncio import AsyncIOScheduler
from app.core.database import AsyncSessionLocal
from app.core.config import settings
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

async def sync_all_calendars():
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as db:
        from sqlalchemy import select
        from app.models.calendar import CalendarSource
        result = await db.execute(
            select(CalendarSource).where(CalendarSource.enabled == True)
        )
        sources = result.scalars().all()
        for source in sources:
            try:
                await sync_one_source(source, db)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error('Sync failed for source %s: %s', source.id, e)
                source.sync_error = str(e)
                await db.commit()

def register_calendar_sync_jobs(scheduler: AsyncIOScheduler):
    scheduler.add_job(
        sync_all_calendars,
        'interval',
        minutes=settings.calendar_sync_interval_minutes,
        id='calendar_sync',
        replace_existing=True
    )

files_to_create.description: OAuth callback routes for all calendar providers.

Create TaskCompletion row. If task has recurrence_rule, call generate_recurring_next_instance(). Emit task_updated to event_bus.

files_to_create.description: check_overdue_tasks() APScheduler cron job running every hour. Updates status=overdue on tasks past due_date with status=pending.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
async def check_overdue_tasks():
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as db:
        from sqlalchemy import update
        from app.models.tasks import Task
        from datetime import datetime, timezone
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        stmt = (
            update(Task)
            .where(Task.due_date < now, Task.status == 'pending', Task.is_deleted == False)
            .values(status='overdue')
        )
        await db.execute(stmt)
        await db.commit()

tasks.title: Photo and album API
tasks.objective: Photo upload (JPEG/PNG/HEIC), Pillow thumbnail generation, album CRUD, slideshow endpoint.

files_to_create.description: Photo processing: save_photo(), generate_thumbnail(), extract_exif_date(). HEIC registration on module load.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
import asyncio
import uuid
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime
from PIL import Image
from PIL.ExifTags import TAGS
import pillow_heif

pillow_heif.register_heif_opener()

async def process_upload(file_data: bytes, original_filename: str) -> dict:
    ext = Path(original_filename).suffix.lower()
    if ext in ('.heic', '.heif'):
        ext = '.jpg'
    photo_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    orig_path = f'/data/photos/originals/{photo_id}{ext}'
    thumb_path = f'/data/photos/thumbnails/{photo_id}_thumb.jpg'
    with open(orig_path, 'wb') as f:
        f.write(file_data)
    await asyncio.to_thread(_resize, orig_path, thumb_path, (400, 300))
    taken_at = await asyncio.to_thread(_get_exif_date, orig_path)
    return {'id': photo_id, 'file_path': orig_path, 'thumbnail_path': thumb_path, 'taken_at': taken_at}

def _resize(src, dst, size):
    with Image.open(src) as img:
        img.thumbnail(size, Image.LANCZOS)
        img.save(dst, 'JPEG', quality=85)

def _get_exif_date(path: str) -> datetime | None:
    try:
        with Image.open(path) as img:
            exif = img._getexif()
            if exif:
                for tag_id, val in exif.items():
                    if TAGS.get(tag_id) == 'DateTimeOriginal':
                        return datetime.strptime(val, '%Y:%m:%d %H:%M:%S')
    except Exception:
        return None

tasks.title: Automated daily backup job
tasks.objective: APScheduler cron job running daily at BACKUP_TIME. SQLite backup via sqlite3.backup() API. Prunes old files beyond BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS.

files_to_create.description: daily_backup() cron job using sqlite3.backup() for safe copy during live operation.
files_to_create.code_pattern:
import sqlite3
import logging
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime
from app.core.config import settings

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

async def daily_backup():
    db_path = Path('/data/db/familyhub.db')
    backup_dir = Path('/data/backups')
    backup_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    date_str = datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    dest = backup_dir / f'familyhub_{date_str}.db'
    try:
        src = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))
        dst = sqlite3.connect(str(dest))
        src.backup(dst)
        src.close()
        dst.close()
        logger.info('Backup created: %s', dest)
        all_backups = sorted(backup_dir.glob('familyhub_*.db'))
        for old in all_backups[:-settings.backup_retention_days]:
            old.unlink()
            logger.info('Pruned old backup: %s', old)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error('Backup failed: %s', e)

changes: In lifespan, register backup job: backup_hour, backup_minute = settings.backup_time.split(':'); scheduler.add_job(daily_backup, 'cron', hour=int(backup_hour), minute=int(backup_minute), id='daily_backup')

exchange_code(code): acquire_token_by_authorization_code()
refresh_token(credentials): acquire_token_by_refresh_token()
sync_events(source): GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/calendarView/delta with delta link for incremental sync
Handle 410 Gone by falling back to full sync
Push event for bidirectional: POST /me/calendars/{id}/events

tasks.title: Apple CalDAV integration
tasks.objective: CalDAV connection to iCloud using app-specific password. Incremental sync via REPORT with time range. Write support via PUT ics objects.

files_to_create.description: Apple CalDAV integration using caldav library.
key_points: connect(email, app_specific_password): caldav.DAVClient('https://caldav.icloud.com', username=email, password=app_specific_password)
discover_calendars(client): client.principal().calendars()
sync_events(source): REPORT with VCALENDAR time range filter, parse icalendar objects
Implement exponential backoff: on HTTP 429/503 wait 2^n seconds up to 5 retries
For write: create vCalendar ics string, caldav.Calendar.save_event(ics)

Notification badge in calendar settings showing count of unresolved conflicts.

files_to_create.description: Script that docker compose pull && docker compose up -d --build, with pre-update backup.

cross_cutting_tasks.description: These run throughout all phases — treat as ongoing obligations not one-time tasks.

items.title: Security review before each phase release
checklist: Every new API endpoint has authentication (get_current_user or explicit allow-list)
Role checks on write operations (require_role used correctly)
No sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PINs) returned in API responses
All user input validated via Pydantic schemas with field constraints
File uploads: validate MIME type, reject non-image files, cap max size

items.title: Test every Alembic migration upgrade AND downgrade before merging
items.command: alembic upgrade head && alembic downgrade -1 && alembic upgrade head

items.title: Pi 4 hardware performance test at end of each phase
checklist: docker stats shows total memory < 300MB idle
p95 API response < 500ms under 5 concurrent users (use Apache Bench: ab -n 200 -c 5)
Dashboard loads in < 3s cold on local network

items.title: Keep .env.example in sync with all env vars used in code
items.rule: Every new settings field added to config.py must be documented in .env.example with a comment
